note: SYNTHESIZED from ST 1.18.0 source, not captured. A disabled `main` is retained as a structural EMPTY prompt (content='') so relative inserts still resolve — ST's 'GMO-free vegan replacement' (PromptManager.js:1531-1537). That empty-content message is then dropped from the FINAL wire array by getChat (openai.js:3737-3745, `if (message.content || message.tool_calls)`), independent of squashing — so the empty main never reaches the model, while later prompts keep their positions. RPT matches this by emitting no wire message for the structural empty main. Adapter-verify at Phase-2 wiring. Prose is RPT-authored + scrambled.

input.description: main disabled in the order (structural empty); a following custom prompt still renders at its position; empty main absent from the wire.

prompts.name: Custom Note
prompts.content: Note-after-main: the quorlish gate stays open.

character.name: Yara
character.description: Character brindle: Yara, a gate-warden with a copper key.

chatMessages.content: Yara, is the gate still open?

mustContain: Note-after-main: the quorlish gate stays open.
